name: Senda de la Cova del Parpalló (SL-CV 79) — circular desde La Drova
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell are set within a protected natural area characterized by mountainous landscapes and diverse Mediterranean vegetation. The terrain features a calcareous hydrological system, resulting in varied geological formations. Hikers can expect to find holm oak forests, scrubland, and pine trees. The region offers a range of trails suitable for different fitness levels. Best family friendly hiking trails in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell The most popular family friendly hiking route is Mirador del Coll de la Rafaela and Cim del Mondúver — circular from La Drova, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and offers expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda de la Cova del Parpalló (SL-CV 79) — circular desde La Drova, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail winds through varied Mediterranean landscapes. Local hikers also love the Caldereta Viewpoint – Coll de la Radella Viewpoint loop from Cova del Parpalló, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail leading through forested areas and offering viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Hiking in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell is defined by mountainous landscapes, Mediterranean vegetation, and varied geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 800 reviews. More than 5,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails can I find in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell?
A: You'll find a wide selection of family-friendly hiking trails in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell, with over 170 routes available. More than 70 of these are classified as easy, making them ideal for families looking for a relaxed outing.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family-friendly hikes in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell?
A: The natural park offers diverse terrain, from easier, well-trodden paths to more challenging sections with uneven and steep ground. Many family-friendly routes are designed to be accessible, but some may require sure-footedness. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes, Mediterranean vegetation, and sometimes rocky paths, so appropriate footwear is always recommended.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes perfect for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352329460/senda-de-la-cova-del-parpallo-sl-cv-79-circular-desde-la-drova" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Cova del Parpalló (SL-CV 79)&lt;/a&gt; is an easier circular option. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14677615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldereta Viewpoint – Coll de la Radella Viewpoint loop from Cova del Parpalló&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful scenery.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking with family in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for family hiking in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, so if you visit then, it's best to start early in the morning or hike in the late afternoon to avoid the midday sun. Winter offers cooler, crisp days, perfect for invigorating walks.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the family-friendly trails?
A: Absolutely! A major highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6221093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parpalló-Borrell Interpretation Centre&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a great starting point. You can also explore the area around the famous Cova del Parpalló, an important archaeological site. Many trails also lead to stunning viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5688912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Miradors&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the diverse landscape.

Q: Can we bring our dog on the family-friendly hiking trails?
A: Many trails in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your furry companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or where wildlife might be present, and to respect local regulations regarding pets. Always carry water for your dog, especially on longer routes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might we spot on a family hike?
A: The park's rich flora supports diverse fauna. Keep an eye out for various bird species, especially in the holm oak forests and scrubland. You might also spot genets, foxes, and wild boars, though these are often more elusive. The Mediterranean vegetation itself, with aromatic herbs like pebrella and thyme, is a treat for the senses.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great views without being too strenuous for children?
A: Yes, several routes provide rewarding views without excessive difficulty. The trails around the Caldereta Viewpoint often offer moderate options with beautiful vistas. For a more challenging but incredibly rewarding experience for older children, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353840613/mirador-del-coll-de-la-rafaela-and-cim-del-monduver-circular-from-la-drova" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Coll de la Rafaela and Cim del Mondúver&lt;/a&gt; circular route provides spectacular 360-degree views from the summit, where on clear days, you can even see Ibiza and Formentera.

Q: Where can we park when visiting the family-friendly hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available at common starting points for trails, such as near the Cova del Parpalló or in the vicinity of La Drova. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for the most convenient parking locations for your chosen trail. Always park responsibly and ensure you don't block access for residents or emergency services.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Paratge Natural Municipal Parpalló-Borrell?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning panoramic views from elevated points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1065176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondúver summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the well-maintained trails that cater to various fitness levels. The historical significance of sites like the Cova del Parpalló also adds a unique dimension to the hiking experience.

Q: Are there any natural springs or water sources along the trails?
A: Yes, the park features natural springs, which can provide refreshing stops for hikers. While these are lovely to encounter, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water for your entire family, especially on longer routes, as the availability of potable water from springs can vary seasonally and is not always guaranteed.
tips: The Parpalló cave is located in La Drova, at the foot of Montdúver, in the municipality of Gandia, La Safor. The cave was intensely inhabited during the Upper Paleolithic period and was one of the first inhabited by Homo sapiens to reach the peninsular Mediterranean region.
